CAPABILITIES BRIEFING CHECKLIST: OSDBU CALLS (how to call agency Small Business / OSDBU representatives and secure a capabilities briefing)

PURPOSE: Establish contact with Small Business representatives and secure an in-person briefing to: build rapport with the agency; learn about future and current opportunities; get introduced to potential government buyers/decision makers; present your company's capabilities and solutions; find integration opportunities with agency teams.

PREPARATION MATERIALS: A finalized capability statement ready to share, and a researched agency forecast list with relevant opportunities identified.

INITIAL CALL PROCESS: Introduce yourself and your company. Request an in-person capabilities briefing. Mention your relevant past performance for projects on their forecast list. Be ready with your elevator pitch if you reach the representative.

VOICEMAIL MESSAGE (if necessary): State your name and company clearly, provide your phone number, mention you'll send a follow-up email with your capabilities statement, keep it brief and professional.

FOLLOW-UP EMAIL: Introduce your company concisely; outline core services/products; mention notable clients or past projects; highlight a distinctive feature (certifications, licenses, specialized equipment); attach your capabilities statement; provide complete point-of-contact information.

PERSISTENT FOLLOW-UP STRATEGY: Call 2–3 times per week at varying times (early morning, late afternoon, alternate days). Leave only one voicemail every two weeks. Track all contact attempts and responses.

POST-CONTACT ACTIONS: Complete all actions the representative recommends (attend suggested events, join recommended organizations, research additional opportunities). Send progress updates via email: new contract awards, contract-vehicle acceptances, organizational memberships, Sources Sought responses, new project discoveries.

NETWORKING STRATEGY: Identify agency events and attend; research where agency reps will be; prepare event-specific materials.

ALTERNATIVE APPROACH (no forecast list): Research what goods/services the agency procures; prepare to discuss your relevant capabilities; request information about upcoming opportunities; still request a capabilities briefing based on general agency needs.

ADDITIONAL NOTES: Be ready for directors to request your capabilities statement for forwarding. Some agencies offer monthly vendor outreach sessions instead of individual briefings. Adapt your approach based on initial feedback.
